title: Cold-Gas Thruster Dynamics and Pulse-Width Modulation Control
content: Cold-gas thrusters expel pressurised inert gas (N2, CO2) through a solenoid valve to generate micro-Newton to milli-Newton thrust. Impulse bit I_bit = F * t_pulse. Thrust is modulated by varying pulse width (PWM duty on solenoid) or pulse repetition frequency. Minimum pulse width is limited by valve response time (~5 ms). Specific impulse Isp ~65 s for N2. Total ΔV budget from Tsiolkovsky: ΔV = Isp * g0 * ln(m0/mf). Used on CubeSats for attitude and orbit control.
